rules.description: Minimum rainwater tank capacity per 100sqm roof catchment
rules.notes: 100sqm roof in 2000mm/yr rainfall zone = 200,000L potential. 5000L tank covers ~2 weeks dry spell for non-potable use. Scale linearly with roof area.

rules.description: First-flush diverter required to discard initial contaminated runoff
rules.notes: First 1-2mm of rainfall washes dust, bird droppings, and debris from roof. Divert first 20L per 100sqm before collection begins.

rules.description: Gutter sizing for tropical rainfall intensity — minimum 150mm half-round
rules.notes: Philippines peak rainfall intensity: 100-200mm/hr. Standard temperate gutters (100mm) overflow. Use 150mm half-round or 125mm box gutters with multiple downspouts.

rules.description: Minimum permeable surface area as percentage of lot
rules.notes: Reduces runoff, recharges groundwater, reduces urban heat island. Use permeable pavers, gravel, grass pavers, or preserved natural ground.

rules.description: Site grading should direct water away from building at minimum slope
rules.notes: 2% minimum slope away from foundation for 3m, then 1% beyond. Prevents foundation saturation in heavy rains.

rules.description: Bioswales or rain gardens for on-site stormwater treatment
rules.notes: Natural filtration of runoff through planted depressions. Size at 5-10% of impervious catchment area. Use native aquatic-tolerant species.

rules.description: Tree canopy coverage target for outdoor comfort
rules.notes: 40% canopy coverage reduces ambient air temp by 2-4°C through evapotranspiration and shade. Position deciduous trees on south/west for seasonal benefit.

rules.description: Shade trees minimum distance from building for root protection
rules.notes: Large trees (mango, acacia, rain tree) need 5-8m. Medium trees (talisay, fire tree) 3-5m. Prevents root damage to foundations while still providing shade.

rules.description: Green wall or vertical garden for west facade heat reduction
rules.notes: Vegetated walls reduce surface temperature by 10-15°C compared to bare concrete. Most effective on west facade which receives harsh afternoon sun.

rules.description: Ground cover between buildings to reduce heat reflection
rules.notes: Bare concrete/asphalt between buildings creates heat canyon effect. Use grass, ground cover plants, or shaded pavers. Reduces reflected heat load on adjacent facades.

rules.description: Priority shade trees for Philippine tropical architecture
rules.value: narra, molave, talisay, kamagong, rain_tree, fire_tree, ilang_ilang
rules.notes: Narra (national tree) — broad canopy, nitrogen fixing. Talisay — excellent coastal shade. Rain tree — massive canopy, fast growing. All are drought and typhoon resistant.

rules.description: Windbreak planting — multi-layer buffer for typhoon protection
rules.notes: Layer 1: low shrubs (1-2m). Layer 2: medium trees (5-10m). Layer 3: tall trees (15m+). Plant on windward side. 50% porosity is optimal — solid walls create turbulence.

rules.description: Edible landscaping integration — food forest layer system
rules.notes: Tropical permaculture: canopy (coconut, mango), understory (cacao, calamansi), shrub (malunggay, chili), herb (lemongrass, ginger), ground cover (camote), vine (upo, ampalaya), root (gabi, kamote). Provides food security and microclimate cooling.
